---
title: "Resource Economics and Token Budgets"
chapter_id: "resource-economics-and-token-budgets"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "tokenmana"
  - "planforge"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "viea"
  - "project_theseus_whitepaper"
  - "coilra_multicoil_rope"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Resource Economics and Token Budgets

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `resource-economics-and-token-budgets` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `tokenmana`, `planforge`, `coherence_exchange`, `simulation_scaling`, `viea`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `coilra_multicoil_rope` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Compute, context, verification, and human attention are scarce resources that the architecture must allocate explicitly.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Ignoring resource economics makes high-quality verification unaffordable and encourages synchronized overload or hidden cost shifts.

## Core Claim

[resource-economics-and-token-budgets.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] The stack should account for token budgets, verification tax, load stability, and risk-adjusted inference value.

## Mechanism

- Track task value, uncertainty, cost of error, and inference cost.
- Use regenerative or budgeted capacity mechanisms where useful.
- Escalate verification when risk justifies the cost.

## Interfaces

- Planning allocates budgets.
- Routing chooses costed specialists.
- Evidence measures cost-quality tradeoffs.

## Invariants

- Budgets do not override protected safety gates.
- High-risk tasks pay verification cost.
- Cost savings are recorded with quality results.

## Failure modes

- Cost-cutting verification away.
- Load-synchronized degradation.
- Resource hoarding by low-value tasks.

## Minimal implementation

A resource ledger with budget, risk, cost, quality, and verification tax fields.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Budget allocation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Risk-adjusted verification test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Load stability scenario | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `tokenmana` | TokenMana | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Regenerative capacity mechanisms for load-stable token pricing. |
| `planforge` | PlanForge | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Planning substrate. Goal-to-execution compilation, hierarchical decomposition, DAG planning, scheduling, intelligence arbitrage. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `project_theseus_whitepaper` | Project Theseus Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Local-first report-driven RMI implementation reference: SymLiquid, SparkStream, Octopus Router, residual escrow, self-evolution gates, Hive runtime, observability. |
| `coilra_multicoil_rope` | CoilRA and MultiCoil RoPE | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Adapter-block, residue/winding, block-cyclic, multicoil, relative RoPE, circulant convolution, cyclic mixer, and parameter-accounting substrate with explicit non-claims. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
